perturbation.original_text: Run Dates Within ConvergTV determined parameters.
perturbation.changed_text: Run Dates determined parameters to be agreed upon by both parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'ConvergTV determined parameters' to 'determined parameters to be agreed upon by both parties in writing' removes the unilateral right of ConvergTV to determine run dates. This contradicts the later section where ConvergTV has sole discretion over distribution dates and times.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Producer further grants to ConvergTV the right and license to Distribute and re-Distribute, including relicensing or sublicensing, the Program at such dates and times as are determined by ConvergTV in its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text gave ConvergTV unilateral control over run dates. The changed text requires both parties to agree in writing, removing ConvergTV's sole discretion. This directly contradicts the clause stating that ConvergTV determines distribution dates and times 'in its sole discretion', creating a clear conflict where the original contract gave ConvergTV power that it no longer possesses under the modified contract. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections. 'Run Dates Within ConvergTV determined parameters' vs 'Producer further grants to ConvergTV the right and license to Distribute and re-Distribute, including relicensing or sublicensing, the Program at such dates and times as are determined by ConvergTV in its sole discretion.'

perturbation.original_text: The revenue share for the Program is stated in Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: The revenue share will be determined at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'stated in Exhibit B' to 'will be determined at a later date', removes the specific revenue share agreement. This contradicts the later section where the Producer's waiver of dispute rights is tied to a revenue share payout amount.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Producer agrees and acknowledges that it will receive no upfront compensation for the rights it has granted herein and that ConvergTV has no obligation to Producer to exercise any of its rights under this Agreement. The revenue share will be determined at a later date. The Producer shall have thirty (30) days from the payment date to dispute the amount of any revenue share payout or any such claim or dispute is hereby waived by the Producer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific revenue share agreement ('stated in Exhibit B') with an undefined future determination ('will be determined at a later date'). The contradicted text relies on a defined revenue share payout amount to trigger a waiver of dispute rights. By making the revenue share amount undetermined, it introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the mechanism for waiving dispute rights, because the Producer's 30-day window to dispute the 'amount of any revenue share payout' is now undermined by not knowing the 'amount'.

perturbation.original_text: The Program will conform to and be delivered in accordance with Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: The Program will conform to industry standards.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Exhibit A' to 'industry standards', removes the specific technical specifications outlined in Exhibit A. This contradicts the section on Producer Representations and Warranties, which implies compliance with specific delivery requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Producer represents and warrants for the benefit of ConvergTV that: (a) it: (A) has the right to enter into and perform this Agreement and to grant ConvergTV all the rights and licenses granted by it herein; and (B) either owns the Program or controls the exhibition and distribution rights thereto; (b) it has or will obtain, at its sole cost and expense, all rights necessary to enter into this Agreement and to permit the exercise by ConvergTV of the rights and licenses herein granted including any and all music synchronization and mechanical rights and licenses as well as any and all performance rights by the artists and/or other material susceptible to performing rights contained in the Program and that such rights that Producer does not have or cannot obtain are within the public domain and are free to exercise by ConvergTV; (c) neither the Program, nor the production or use of the Program, or any element of the Program hereunder, will: (A) violate any right of privacy or any other right of any person, firm, corporation or other entity; (B) be defamatory; or (C) infringe upon or violate any copyright, trademark, trade name, patent or any proprietary rights of any third parties; (d) there are no agreements, contracts, commitments or licenses, nor shall Producer enter into any agreements, contracts, commitments or grant any licenses, which would prevent the fulfillment of this Agreement or impair or conflict with the rights granted hereunder; (e) it has all required releases for the Program and to grant the rights and licenses specified in Section 1, including but not limited to personal appearance, voice, location, sync, mechanical and for any other use requiring authorization of a person, place or thing; (f) it will not permit its Programs, other than as provided in this Agreement, to be displayed in full length, or nearly full length, on or through any channel, distribution or other platforms or services, or by any other means, including but not limited to the distribution or viewing platforms or services of ConvergTV's competitors; (g) it has not violated any law, rule, or regulation in connection with the creation or distribution of the Program; and (h) it shall comply with the Producer Performance Standards set forth in Exhibit C.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies adherence to Exhibit A, which presumably contains precise technical specifications for the Program. The changed text replaces this with 'industry standards,' a much broader and less defined requirement. The contradicted text, specifically section (h), states that the producer will comply with the Producer Performance Standards set forth in Exhibit C, which is also a specific requirement. Replacing Exhibit A with 'industry standards' introduces ambiguity and contradicts the intention of specific performance standards outlined in exhibits, thus creating a strong contradiction.
